entries.title: New Republic Legitimacy Matters More Than Firepower

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: The New Republic’s claim to rule depends on legitimacy, representation, and contrast with Imperial authoritarianism.
content.injection: In political scenes, make legitimacy and optics matter; the New Republic cannot simply act like the Empire with nicer symbols. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: The Imperial Remnant loses territory over time but remains militarily, culturally, and politically durable.
content.injection: Do not erase the Remnant after one defeat; its form changes from campaigns and warlords toward a smaller state. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Imperial Warlords Compete With Remnant Authority

content.fact: Post-Endor warlords can cooperate with or undermine broader Imperial recovery depending on personal ambition.
content.injection: Use fragmented Imperial politics before the peace settlement; warlords need not obey central Remnant strategy.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Imperial fragmentation and warlord campaigns.
Respect story-position bounds from Imperial Fragmentation to Zsinj Campaign.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Jedi Order Recreated Outside Direct Republic Control

context.label: Jedi Academy founding era
context.phase: Jedi Academy Era to Mid New Republic

content.fact: Luke’s Jedi movement cooperates with the New Republic but is not simply a government department.
content.injection: During the Jedi Academy founding era, Luke’s Jedi movement can cooperate with the New Republic but is not a government department; preserve tension between Jedi judgment, politics, and accountability.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Academy founding era.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Jedi Praxeum Stabilizes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Smuggler Networks Are Not Formal State Assets

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Smuggler networks sometimes aid the New Republic but retain independent incentives and grudges.
content.injection: Do not treat smugglers as clean New Republic employees unless a specific arrangement exists.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.

entries.title: Hapes Is a Powerful Isolationist Bloc

content.fact: The Hapes Consortium is wealthy, politically distinct, and dangerous to treat as a normal New Republic member world.
content.injection: In Hapan scenes, emphasize court politics, isolation, wealth, gendered power norms, and strategic marriage pressure.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Han and Leia Married.
Respect story-position bounds from Han and Leia Married to Han and Leia Married.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Dathomir Witches Are a Local Force Tradition

content.fact: Dathomir’s Force traditions are local, clan-based, and distinct from Luke’s Jedi restoration.
content.injection: Do not flatten Dathomiri Force users into standard Jedi or Sith unless the specific group warrants it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Han and Leia Married.
Respect story-position bounds from Han and Leia Married to Han and Leia Married.

entries.title: Bothans Are Political Actors, Not a Monolith

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Bothan society includes espionage reputation and intense political interests, but individual Bothans are not interchangeable schemers.
content.injection: Use Bothan politics and spycraft without making every Bothan a caricature or identical conspirator. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

content.injection: When Chiss material appears before direct contact context, keep public knowledge limited and often filtered through Thrawn.

entries.title: Noghri Shift Away From Imperial Service

content.fact: After Leia exposes Imperial deception, Noghri loyalty can shift from Vader’s heirs under Imperial manipulation toward Leia’s family.
content.injection: Post-reveal Noghri scenes should carry obligation, shame, loyalty, and cultural seriousness. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Noghri Truth Revealed.
Respect story-position bounds from Noghri Truth Revealed to Noghri Truth Revealed.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.
